mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the federal funds rate from elevated levels signals monetary policy pivot toward accommodation. This reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations and lowers refinancing costs for corporates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by more than 0.5 percentage points within 30 calendar days
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.65% on 2020-03-01 reflects emergency rate cuts in response to COVID-19 shock
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following demand shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines >0.5pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. This depresses aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% on 2020-03-01, about to spike due to COVID-19 lockdowns
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.3pp in one month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth reflects consumer confidence and discretionary spending capacity. Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS signals robust demand momentum that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive 5-day returns as the market reprices earnings upward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 483,066M on 2020-03-01 before COVID-19 demand collapse
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 5-day forward returns is below 0.01 over any rolling 1-year window, or correlation reverses sign in more than 40% of rolling 3-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production captures real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing health. A sharp decline in INDPRO signals supply-side contraction and reduced productive capacity, which constrains earnings growth and signals recession risk, producing negative 20-day returns as equity risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.66 on 2020-03-01, fell to 97.41 by actual revision reflecting COVID-19 production shutdowns
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances where INDPRO declines >2% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and business confidence in future demand. When both series decline simultaneously, it indicates broad-based demand destruction across residential and consumption channels, producing negative 60-day returns as the market reprices recession probability upward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines >5% MoM AND RSAFS declines >3% MoM in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,599K on 2020-02-01, fell to 1,549K by revision; RSAFS fell from 483,066M to 468,324M
regime_hint: demand shock with synchronized contraction across housing and consumption
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 60% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS decline simultaneously by stated thresholds, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly characterize labor market tightness. When PAYEMS declines sharply while UNRATE rises, it signals rapid labor market deterioration and forward earnings risk, producing negative 20-day returns as equity risk premiums expand in response to recession signals.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines >500K in one month AND UNRATE rises >0.5pp in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151,786K on 2020-03-01, fell to 150,895K; UNRATE at 4.4% about to spike to 14.7% by April 2020
regime_hint: labor market shock with rapid employment destruction
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 65% of instances where PAYEMS declines >500K and UNRATE rises >0.5pp simultaneously, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains subdued, monetary policy is constrained at the zero lower bound. In this regime, further monetary accommodation cannot occur, and equity returns depend on fiscal stimulus and supply-side recovery. Positive returns require INDPRO recovery signals.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL growth < 0.2% MoM AND INDPRO rises >1% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.65% on 2020-03-01; CPIAUCSL at 257.95 with low inflation; INDPRO at 103.66 before collapse
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary regime with supply-side recovery focus
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances where all three trigger conditions are met, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.01 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) is the broadest measure of aggregate demand. When GDPC1 growth turns negative or declines sharply from prior quarter, it signals recession onset and earnings contraction, producing negative 60-day returns as the market reprices growth expectations and risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines quarter-over-quarter or growth rate falls below -1% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,221.97B on 2019-10-01, revised to 20,985.45B; Q1 2020 GDP contracted sharply due to COVID-19
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances where GDPC1 declines QoQ or annualized growth < -1%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.
